#REDACTED, Posted: Dec 26 2002 at 3:46 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I got these for my 54 druid , I love the 20 ac and the 35 mana,,, I do use es gaunts in my inventory and have those and es vambs set up to quick change when I run out of mana or killing a low level mob( Ok FARMING ) there I said it .. I am so ashamed.. These make the difference in getting off that gate as well.. I have 228 wisdom but I am in the process of getting pos mana items and keeping my wis the same . I was told that the new cap is 255 wis but I see far more of an improvment in overall mana with pos mana items than wis items at 200 plus... any one know the answer conclusivly

QuoteReply
# Aug 29 2002 at 2:35 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592759392552
Scholar
627 posts
but my rogue won't be using them for now since I'm using SCHW. I'll toss them on my semi-twink bard for the time being. I'm not going to bother giving them to my 40 druid. Yeah, the AC is nice but if you're good at aggro control you won't be getting hit often.
▲ Page top
please consider this
QuoteReply
# Jul 16 2002 at 5:13 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591776332560
9 posts
To the people who say Druids shouldnt't roll on these gloves-

First of all, 20ac is good for melee classes at this level. For a druid, clad in mostly leather class armor, it is godlike. This is some of the best armor druids can get and I have seen many wear it 55+. The melee have mostly gotten better by then. This is pretty much the only decent ac armor druids can get before 55. Also 35 mana is very nice, especially at the level when most have 200+ wisdom, and in a slot that historically has lacked mana/wis for the druid class.

Why would a druid want ac? Well, if he is hunting in Velks, chances are, he's a grouping druid, not a soloing druid. Grouping druids get aggro just like other casters. Dots and snares can generate quite a bit of hate, not to mention attempts at ghetto crowd control. Sometimes, druids need to take over the role of the main healer (either because there isn't a cleric, they died, or they are getting beaten on. Clerics can manage the aggro from healing fairly well wearing plate armor and using invulnerability spells. Druids can't.

I agree that if all the tanks, hybrids, and other priests dont have better, everyone should roll. There is no reason why a druid shouldn't roll. Much exp and raid time is spent indoors by grouping druids, where they can't always use speed to escape aggro.
▲ Page top
RE: please consider this
QuoteReply
# Dec 18 2003 at 7:56 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592759261266
Sage
52 posts
Quote:

I can tell you that if these dropped, and the main tank needed them you wouldn't be rolling for JACK sh*t.


(N.B. I apologise for posting above my intended reply. My reply is meant for the post below mine, not the one above mine. Sorry).

For one thing, the person clearly said that if the melee's and hybrids did not have better, it should go to them, and if they did have better, then it was fair to give to the druid, and I have to agree with that. I see nothing wrong with that.

As someone who has a level 40+ Ranger, Pally, Druid and Monk-- your argument is very flawed. Often, my druid (who is on another account) is left on as healer to my hybrid Ranger and Pally. The number of times the druid has gotten aggro from healing us is numerous. The amount of times they've died, is plentiful. So druids do get aggro, and very often, too. They also die real quick. If you are a disgruntled melee basing your argument on solely grouping with druids, I advise you to make one and not judge before you get them to a relatively high level. After all, if we all had alts of every class, we might even learn to tolerate each others class differences and faults.

Secondly, if you're using a NBG (Need Before Greed) system, then if anyone needs the item more than the druid (namely melee's, hybrids, etc), who does not have better, they of course would get the item, and that is fair enough. But if they do, and the next in line to use is the druid, and the druid is going to use the item, there is no reason why the druid should not get it.

If, on the other hand, you're using a FFA (Free For All) looting system then there is no reason why a druid shouldn't roll on it-- and the same with all party members, even people who can't use it -- because you're taking in the value of the item as a whole and not to a particular class or race. Therefore, a pure free for all roll is for everyone, but that is a different matter, and not, what the original poster said at all.

As it is, I bought mine, and that solves the whole big problem about who gets what.


(P.S. Again, I apologise for posting above my intended reply. My reply is meant for the post below mine, not the one above mine.)

Edited, Thu Dec 18 08:03:32 2003
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jan 09 2003 at 6:32 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) People like you **** me off. Compared to other classes, druids RARELY get aggro. Your entire argument over rolling for these gloves is based upon the ac/mana. You would honestly roll on this item when a melee could equip it? Another classic idiot who feels he should have his "fair share" of items, ******** melee out of a good shot at these. I can tell you that if these dropped, and the main tank needed them you wouldn't be rolling for JACK ****.
RE: please consider this
QuoteReply
# Feb 23 2003 at 12:23 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591750863733
36 posts
People who don't know what the hell they're talking about **** me off. Rarely get aggro, eh? You obviously have not played a druid past level 10. Snares and heals can really **** mobs off, no matter how powerful they are. I don't know how many times I would have died because some worthless tank couldn't get a mob off me (and it should be easy since I "never aggro anything") if I didn't take the time to have decent ac and hp items, such as this. Druids have just as much right to it, And that's coming from somebody who has played a warrior and a druid to 50. If you're just mad because you can't even afford these gloves go whine somewhere else. And god knows how many times a tank rolls on some caster item that they couldn't possibly use at all, just to go sell it at the bazaar.

all u guys whining about nbg how they shouldnt go to a druid should keep quiet. at the end of the day, if group agrees nbg loot rules then fine, if not then thats the group decision. if u dont like it u can go look for another group. btw i am a warrior although i have a druid and i would love to have this for him cos all he got are split paw gloves. ac is good for warriors, but its good for everyone else too.

▲ Page top
?
QuoteReply
# Feb 05 2002 at 6:33 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591763939769
168 posts
I know I shouldn't bother to say anything, but I ... just ... can't ... resist! ^_^

- - - - -

Anytime I form a group, I call for a consensus of all the current members on loot rules. Anytime I join a group, I ask "/g What loot rules?" <I should just hotkey that ^_^ >

When you are grouped with like 2 to 5 other strangers (presumably outside of your guild), whatever loot rules you have consented to among the members of that group apply. End of story.

If you and the other strangers decide on NBG, good.

If you and the other strangers decide on random of all droppables, good.

If you and the other strangers decide on "Ninja Loot Everything", good.

Alpha Loot? Master Loot? "Killing Blow" Loot? Whatever is agreed upon is good.

The members of your group are in NO WAY obligated to just hand over a handsome piece of treasure to the most deserving or to the party member who can make the most use of the item. Remember, they are strangers who a) don't know you and b) are ALLOWING you to be with them in their xp/farming endeavors.

Half the time, the ones whining about "Need Before Greed!" are the same dishonest sh*theads trying to pull a fast one. Everyone is a comedian, weatherman, critic and con artist. Trust no one.

So what if the party cleric won 3 Lamentations, or the warrior won that spiffy Int +20 robe? The loot is your / their just reward for risking life and limb in the first place. In the end, it doesn't matter who wins the loot ... sooner or later, it WILL wind up on a character who'll make full usage of it.

So, make sure you are clear on the agreed-upon loot rules when first joining / forming a party. People who are not happy with the loot rules of a group should simply and quietly leave said group.

Khemosaabe "SPAM" Longbow (of Cazic Thule server)
Lvl 54 Slayer-of-Bunny-Wabbits
"/auc WTT my account for Meridian 59 characters!"
▲ Page top
RE: ?
QuoteReply

▲ Page top
Graphics on these for Half Elf Ranger
QuoteReply
# Oct 21 2002 at 4:46 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592252462989
View User Forum
Sage
211 posts
Hello dear

I can only tell you that they look yellow in color on my hands. I would presume, from my faded memory of me wearing CC arms long ago, that a half-elf in CC Armour would look very much like a plate class. The armour if I recall right is a shiny reflection, softer yellow-gold in colour, and would look rather snappy as a complete set.

However, I think it might prove to be rather difficult to collect an entire set of this armour. Other than the gloves, shoes and the arms, the rest of the armur drops in areas that are less frequently camped.

If you do manage to collect an entire suit shoes, arms gloves BP and legs, I would suggest you take a snapshot and send it here.

Happy hunting

QuoteReply
# Aug 18 2001 at 2:08 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592245846566
176 posts
SHM want this item as well.... its the best AC item in hands slot for SHM in the game save for ToV, planar, and other very high end stuff. By very high end i mean around 58-60. These its feasible to buy in EC by the time you are 40. And if not bought then killed by anyone 49+
▲ Page top
Great Druid Gauntlets
QuoteReply
# Aug 14 2001 at 4:04 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591755668273
View User Forum
1 post
Some of you may whine because some of us druids have friends or guildmates that actually don't mind their druids having AC armor. I was given mine by a guildmate that offered them up through guild chat to anyone in the guild that needed them. Being a druid (hence crappy AC gear) I jumped at the opportunity and none of my other guildmates minded at all. Heck, they all get decent AC armor.

These are some of the best AC guantlets available: far better than almost any that are druid only. With the +35 mana I had no problem getting rid of my Splitpaw gloves. Combine these gauntlets with my Icepaw War Claw that the same person gave me from the same raid, and sometimes people don't even recognize me as a druid.

It really is nice to have guild that cares more about the quality of the items it's members are wearing than they are about the amount of money they could make off an item that they don't need.

So, all you people that don't think druids should have AC, remeber that when you need to be evac'd but we can't get the spell off because we are getting wacked to death before you pull the MoB off.

Aerodol Zephyrmoor
47 Druid
Bristlebane
▲ Page top
RE: Great Druid Gauntlets
QuoteReply
# Oct 12 2001 at 7:47 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592249656053
16 posts

__DEL__1593361628546

11 posts
"I can't possibly understand a druid ever "Needing" these more than a melee or enough to be deserving of a roll."

Are you insane? Anyone who is of the level to BE in that area has EVERY right to roll on those. AC is needed by everyone. If these were actually wearable by any other class not included I would have no problem with them equiping these. And i can guarantee that if you actually had the ordasity to say such a thing that you would quickly find yourself one less party member, possibly more since Clerics and Shaman are not MELEE either.
▲ Page top
RE: Great Druid Gauntlets
QuoteReply
# Oct 12 2001 at 7:47 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249656053
16 posts
I think I might tend to disagree on this one. If more of your tanks had these gauntlets then maybe you'd be evacing less often. You obtained them in a fair manner, but in a group rolling on these, I can't possibly understand a druid ever "Needing" these more than a melee or enough to be deserving of a roll.
▲ Page top
RE: Great Druid Gauntlets
QuoteReply
# Oct 12 2001 at 7:47 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592249656053
16 posts
OK, let me clarify. If all the melees, clerics, and high agro people in a group have sufficient AC in the hand slot, then a Druid should have every right to roll on an item such as this. But to take an item from a deserving player who has a MUCH greater need for AC is only self defeating in the long run. In addition, I don't know any intelligent casters who would want to have higher AC at the expense of their front line so that they would run a greater risk of needing an evac or a Res. I guess this comes from an experience of playing in a NBG (Need Before Greed) manner, which you seem to be lacking from your comment,

"Anyone who is of the level to BE in that area has EVERY right to roll"

I wouldn't suggest playing in a selfish manner for long, you'll find the number of your friends in EQ shrinking and the number of groups refusing you admittance growing.

Edited, Fri Dec 28 07:24:44 2001
▲ Page top
Best AC gloves
QuoteReply
# Aug 09 2001 at 5:18 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591763446526
48 posts
I solo'd this mob. Fight was tough, but I won and these dropped. These are some of the best AC gloves in the game that dont come off of mobs in North ToV or from Veeshans Peek.

I was wearing Coldain Skin Gloves. Those can be sold now.
